panels.description: Webhook ingress throughput. No traffic in the selected window should stay at 0, not No data.
panels.title: Webhook Receive Rate (QPS by Source)

panels.description: Application-side active webhook event count gauge.
panels.title: Active Alert Records (Active DB Records)
API Request Rate (HTTP Rate by Route/Status)

panels.description: Consumer pending and lag are the real backlog signals. Redis Stream retained entries (XLEN) may keep growing until maxlen trimming and should not be read as unconsumed work.
panels.title: Queue Consumer Backlog (Pending / Lag / Retained)
Worker Task Duration P95 (Worker Task Duration)

panels.description: Webhook storm/backpressure suppressions over the selected range.
panels.title: Alert Storm Suppression (Storm Suppression)

panels.description: Higher values mean a scheduled task has not succeeded recently. No data means the task has not emitted this gauge yet.
panels.title: Scheduler Time Since Last Success
Noise Reduction Trend (Noise Reduction by Relation)

panels.description: If no AI analysis ran during the rate interval, histogram quantile may be empty or NaN.
panels.title: AI Response Latency (AI P95/P50)
Outbox Oldest Backlog Age (Oldest Backlog Age)

panels.description: Firing/pending status of webhookwise-alerts in Prometheus rule_files.
panels.title: Alert Firing Status (Prometheus Alerts)

panels.description: Confirms that business events and webhook status changes on traces/spans are still being produced continuously.
panels.title: Correlation Verification (Logs / Traces)

panels.description: Multi-window SLO error budget burn rate for API / ingress / processing / forward. Fast alerts use 5m+1h, slow alerts use 30m+6h.
panels.title: SLO Burn Rate (Error Budget)
